type_definitions.name: ImplPlanPresenceError
type_definitions.description: Error enum that previously enforced the is_activated() ↔ impl-plan.json present invariant. Removed in T025 because the invariant conflicted with /track:init branch creation (the branch is materialised before impl-plan.json is authored, which turned Phase 0-2 progression into a corruption false-positive). `derive_track_status` now returns Planned via its graceful fallback when impl-plan.json is absent. Catalogue entry kept with `action: delete` so the baseline reverse check acknowledges the intentional deletion until the next baseline recapture.

type_definitions.name: SpecRefFinding
type_definitions.description: Structured finding produced by the domain pure function check_catalogue_spec_ref_integrity. Holds the layer id, optional catalogue entry name, optional spec_refs index, optional spec file path, and a SpecRefFindingKind discriminant. Emitted as Vec<SpecRefFinding> and consumed by the CLI layer for human-readable stderr formatting (D1.5). I/O-free — the struct is a plain data carrier in libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue_spec_signal.rs.

type_definitions.name: SpecRefFindingKind
type_definitions.description: Enum discriminating the three categories of catalogue spec-ref integrity violations. Each variant carries associated diagnostic data per ADR D1.5: DanglingAnchor { anchor: SpecElementId } (SpecRef.anchor absent from spec.json; carries the failing anchor for CLI error output), HashMismatch { anchor: SpecElementId, declared: ContentHash, actual: ContentHash } (SpecRef.hash differs from canonical SHA-256 of spec element subtree; carries anchor + declared vs actual hashes for diff output), StaleSignals { declared_catalogue_hash: ContentHash, actual_catalogue_hash: ContentHash } (catalogue_declaration_hash in signals file differs from current layer-types.json SHA-256; layer-level finding, carries both hashes). Enum-first per 04-coding-principles.md — three fixed variants with no transitions. Defined in libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue_spec_signal.rs alongside SpecRefFinding.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueSpecSignal
type_definitions.description: Per-entry signal record in the <layer>-catalogue-spec-signals.json output. Fields: type_name (String) and signal (ConfidenceSignal — blue / yellow / red). Produced by applying the informal-priority rule (D1.1) to a single TypeCatalogueEntry. 1 entry → 1 CatalogueSpecSignal; multiple spec_refs[] per entry are collapsed into 1 signal. Defined in libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue_spec_signal.rs.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueSpecSignalsDocument
type_definitions.description: Aggregate root for <layer>-catalogue-spec-signals.json. Schema v1 fields: schema_version (1), catalogue_declaration_hash (SHA-256 hex of the input <layer>-types.json for stale detection), signals (Vec<CatalogueSpecSignal>). No generated_at field — deterministic output, byte-identical across multiple runs with the same input. Decoded by the infrastructure CatalogueSpecSignals codec; consumed by VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsInteractor and check_catalogue_spec_signals. Defined in libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue_spec_signal.rs.
